forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: ac80
+project: diagnostics
+title: Forward inline diagnostics to the editor gutter via ACP session/update
+---
+## What
+llama-agent owns the loop, so it can deliver diagnostics two ways: (1) the write op's result already feeds straight back into model context for free (`agent.rs` appends `ToolResult.result` JSON to the conversation) — no work needed; and (2) forward the same diagnostics as an ACP `session/update` to the editor's native gutter (e.g. Xcode). This task does (2).
+
+- After a mutating tool call returns inline diagnostics (from the fold-in step), llama-agent broadcasts a diagnostics `SessionNotification` over the existing `broadcast::Sender<SessionNotification>` (`acp/server.rs:59,320`; builders in `acp/translation.rs:611`). Add a diagnostics-carrying `SessionUpdate` path — either a dedicated variant if the ACP schema supports one, or structured diagnostics in the existing notification envelope — so the editor can render gutter markers.
+- Mirror the existing plan/mode notification pattern (`send_plan_notification_from_result` extracts `_plan` from a tool result and broadcasts) — extract diagnostics from the tool result similarly and broadcast.
+
+## Depends on
+- "Inline-on-edit: mutated_paths + shared diagnostics fold-in step"
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] After an instrumented edit, llama-agent emits a diagnostics `session/update` over the broadcast channel carrying the structured report.
+- [ ] The model-context path is verified to already carry diagnostics (no regression) — diagnostics in `ToolResult.result` reach the conversation.
+- [ ] No second analysis: the ACP forward reuses the report already in the tool result.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `cargo test -p llama-agent`: a fake tool result containing a diagnostics field drives `generate()`/the broadcast path; assert a diagnostics `SessionNotification` is published AND the tool result is appended to the session messages. Use the scripted fake-model seam (no GPU/weights), <10s.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd`. Reuse the plan/mode notification extraction pattern; do not add a parallel channel.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,6 +6,19 @@
   id: 01kvee6s5pxxzjbsr3fq30cn48
   text: 'Picked up by /finish $diagnostics (scoped-batch), after ^cxz8vs4 → done. First ready by ordinal. Dependency satisfied: inline-on-edit fold-in landed (10ea03149). Scope: after a mutating tool call returns inline diagnostics, llama-agent broadcasts a diagnostics SessionNotification over the EXISTING broadcast::Sender<SessionNotification>, mirroring send_plan_notification_from_result (_plan extraction) — extract diagnostics from the tool result similarly and broadcast as a SessionUpdate. Reuse the report already in the tool result (no second analysis); verify model-context path already carries diagnostics in ToolResult.result (no regression). Test via the scripted fake-model seam (<10s, no GPU/weights). Driving implement → test → review.'
   timestamp: 2026-06-18T22:39:10.518352+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kveeh51qcq82s8hhg323hk8j
+  text: |-
+    Research done. Key findings:
+
+    PATTERN TO MIRROR: send_plan_notification_from_result (acp/server.rs) extracts `_plan` from tool_result.result (Value or JSON-string), builds SessionUpdate::Plan, calls broadcast_notification. Wired at the tool-result site (after append_tool_result, gated on tool_name=="mcp__sah__kanban"). I'll mirror with send_diagnostics_notification_from_result extracting `diagnostics`/`pending`.
+
+    SESSIONUPDATE-SHAPE DECISION: agent-client-protocol-schema 0.13.2 SessionUpdate is #[non_exhaustive] and has NO diagnostics variant (variants: UserMessageChunk, AgentMessageChunk, AgentThoughtChunk, ToolCall, ToolCallUpdate, Plan, AvailableCommandsUpdate, CurrentModeUpdate, ConfigOptionUpdate, SessionInfoUpdate, +unstable UsageUpdate). So per the card's fallback: STRUCTURED DIAGNOSTICS IN THE EXISTING NOTIFICATION ENVELOPE — attach the report under the SessionNotification `_meta` (the ACP-blessed extension field) via .meta(), exactly as build_replay_notification already does. Base update = ToolCallUpdate (the gutter-relevant carrier tied to the originating call) so a diagnostics-aware editor can map markers to the file.
+
+    FOLD-IN SHAPE (swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/inline_diagnostics.rs, commit 10ea03149): diagnostics ride on the MCP CallToolResult as (a) structured_content["diagnostics"]/["pending"] and (b) an APPENDED text block `{"diagnostics": <report>, "pending": <bool>}`. The report is swissarmyhammer_diagnostics::DiagnosticsReport (serde: counts, diagnostics[]).
+
+    BLOCKER DISCOVERY (the card's path-1 "already free" premise is WRONG): llama-agent's UnifiedMCPClient::call_tool (mcp.rs) reduces the whole CallToolResult to `content.first()` text only — it drops structured_content AND the appended diagnostics text block. So diagnostics currently reach NEITHER the conversation (path 1) NOR tool_result.result (needed for path-2 extraction). Enabling fix (minimal, only diagnostics sets structured_content anywhere in swissarmyhammer-tools, so low blast radius): when CallToolResult.structured_content is present, call_tool surfaces it into the returned String so tool_result.result carries the diagnostics. This fixes path 1 (no fold-in rebuild) and enables path-2 extraction. Proceeding via /tdd.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-18T22:44:50.359200+00:00
 position_column: doing
 position_ordinal: '8180'
 project: diagnostics

   timestamp: 2026-06-18T22:44:50.359200+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kvefg8axyq550b8knsndqrw0
+  text: |-
+    Implementation landed via /tdd (RED watched fail, then GREEN):
+
+    FILES + SYMBOLS:
+    - crates/llama-agent/src/acp/translation.rs: new diagnostics_notification(session_id, call_id, report, pending) — builds SessionNotification with SessionUpdate::ToolCallUpdate base + structured {diagnostics, pending} in the _meta envelope (no diagnostics variant in #[non_exhaustive] ACP SessionUpdate; _meta per card fallback, mirrors build_replay_notification).
+    - crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs: new send_diagnostics_notification_from_result — mirrors send_plan_notification_from_result; extracts `diagnostics`/`pending` from tool_result.result (Value-object OR JSON-string), broadcasts via the EXISTING broadcast_notification/notification_tx (no parallel channel), no-op when absent. Wired at the tool-result site after append_tool_result, NOT tool-name-gated (any mutator may carry diagnostics; extractor self-gates).
+    - crates/llama-agent/src/mcp.rs: enabling fix — extracted result_to_string/first_text_content from UnifiedMCPClient::call_tool. Surfaces CallToolResult.structured_content (only the inline-diagnostics fold-in sets it anywhere in swissarmyhammer-tools, so low blast radius) as JSON into ToolResult.result, preserving original text under `content`. This makes path-1 (conversation) carry diagnostics AND enables path-2 extraction. Previously call_tool dropped everything but content.first().
+
+    TESTS (5 new, all <1s, model-free scripted seam): mcp::tests::result_to_string_{returns_first_text_when_unstructured,surfaces_structured_diagnostics}; acp::server::tests::diagnostics_forward::{diagnostics_result_is_broadcast_as_notification, result_without_diagnostics_broadcasts_nothing, diagnostics_result_is_appended_to_session_messages}. Fixtures use the EXACT fold-in shape (DiagnosticsReport: diagnostics[]+counts, wrapped {diagnostics, pending}).
+
+    VERIFICATION: cargo test -p llama-agent --lib = 1113 passed / 0 failed (6.35s); cargo clippy -p llama-agent --all-targets -- -D warnings = 0 warnings; cargo fmt --check clean. (Full `cargo test -p llama-agent` includes pre-existing real-model integration tests that load Qwen weights / run ~9min — the known no-fake-seam caveat; new coverage is in --lib, <10s.)
+
+    Adversarial double-check running.
+  timestamp: 2026-06-18T23:01:49.533651+00:00

+  text: |-
+    Review (2026-06-18): CLEAN — moved to done. Engine reported 2 blockers + 3 warnings (1/15 review tasks failed → INCOMPLETE), all verified by direct inspection:
+
+    REFUTED — both "blockers" are hallucinations from the failed review task: (1) "duplicate definition of send_diagnostics_notification_from_result @1338" and (2) "orphaned code @1400". grep shows the fn is defined exactly ONCE (server.rs:1141, 3 call sites); lines 1338/1400 are normal load_session/resume_session code. `cargo build -p llama-agent --lib` Finished exit 0 — the file compiles; no duplicate, no orphaned braces.
+
+    WARNINGS (advisory, non-blocking): process_tool_call length = pre-existing complexity, not this delta; "diagnostics"/"pending" string keys could be consts (minor; cross-crate contract); result_to_string "reimplements" CLI format_success_response (0.87 probe) — REFUTED as regression: different crates/layers (CLI YAML display vs agent JSON conversation threading), not the same capability.
+
+    BLAST RADIUS of mcp.rs structured_content surfacing (the load-bearing concern): LOW, no regression. Repo-wide grep confirms the ONLY producer that SETS structured_content on a CallToolResult is swissarmyhammer-tools/inline_diagnostics.rs (the fold-in); no tool uses rmcp typed-return auto-structuring; mcp_integration.rs:188 only READS it for CLI display. result_to_string early-returns prior first_text_content when structured_content is absent, so every non-diagnostics result is threaded byte-for-byte as before. Implementer's claim verified.
+
+    OTHER DIMENSIONS confirmed: SessionUpdate (schema 0.13.2) is #[non_exhaustive] with NO diagnostics variant → _meta on SessionNotification via .meta() is the right ACP mechanism (mirrors build_replay_notification); ToolCallUpdate base ties markers to the originating call_id; reuses existing notification_tx, mirrors send_plan_notification_from_result (no 2nd channel, no re-analysis); extractor/fold-in shapes match end-to-end (top-level diagnostics/pending; severity "Error" canonical form). 5 model-free tests pass fresh: 3 broadcast/append @0.67s + 2 result_to_string @0.00s, <10s.
